issues.summary: Migrate EMR cluster into TFE on stage environment
issues.description:
Need to migrate old terraform code into TFE version. In this task I will be dealing with EMR clusters in the STAGE environment.  
  
Edit: The migration turned out to be more complicated than expected. So we had to rebuild the module from scratch, incorporating the fixes that came out during the failure BIM-583. Below is a description of all the changes.  

```
PART 3 - Building EMR Clusters in TFE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Location: environments/staging/us-east-1/ (TFE workspace)                                                                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Module: modules/emr_new/ (new module, replaces modules/emr/)                                                                                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  3.1 - Replacing terraform_remote_state with data sources                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Problem: The module was reading VPC/network data from an S3 state file, creating a dependency on the old backend.                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Old code (removed):                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  data "terraform_remote_state" "network" {                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
    backend = "s3"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
    config = {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
      bucket = var.network_state_s3_bucket                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
      key    = var.network_state_s3_key                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
      region = var.remote_region                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
    }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  New code (added to main.tf):
  data "aws_s3_bucket" "emr"              { bucket = var.emr_bucket_name }
  data "aws_key_pair" "emr"               { key_name = var.key_pair_name }
  data "aws_subnets" "private"            { filter vpc-id + tag Tier=private }
  data "aws_subnet" "private"             { for_each = toset(data.aws_subnets.private.ids) }
  data "aws_security_group" "emr_master"  { name = var.emr_master_sg_name, vpc_id = var.vpc_id }
  data "aws_security_group" "emr_slave"   { name = var.emr_slave_sg_name,  vpc_id = var.vpc_id }
  data "aws_security_group" "emr_service" { name = var.emr_service_sg_name, vpc_id = var.vpc_id }
  data "aws_security_group" "ec2_jumpbox" { name = var.ec2_jumpbox_sg_name, vpc_id = var.vpc_id }

  # Subnets sorted by AZ for deterministic ordering
  locals {
    private_subnet_ids = [for s in sort([for id, s in data.aws_subnet.private : "${s.availability_zone}:${s.id}"]) : split(":", s)[1]]
  }

  Removed variables:
  remote_region            # removed
  network_state_s3_bucket  # removed
  network_state_s3_key     # removed

  Added variables:
  variable "vpc_id"              { type = string }
  variable "emr_bucket_name"     { type = string }
  variable "key_pair_name"       { type = string }
  variable "emr_master_sg_name"  { type = string }
  variable "emr_slave_sg_name"   { type = string }
  variable "emr_service_sg_name" { type = string }
  variable "ec2_jumpbox_sg_name" { type = string }

  Values for staging:
  vpc_id              = "vpc-05f842439661a5939"
  emr_bucket_name     = "adgear-risuwon-us-east-1"
  key_pair_name       = "risuwon-20210408175150767000000001"
  emr_master_sg_name  = "risuwon_master_sg"
  emr_slave_sg_name   = "risuwon_slave_sg"
  emr_service_sg_name = "risuwon_service_sg"
  ec2_jumpbox_sg_name = "risuwon_ec2_jumpbox_sg"

  ---
  3.2 - EMR version 7.12.0

  Change in cluster file:
  emr_releaselabel = "emr-7.12.0"  # was emr-6.15.0

  Updated validation regex in variables.tf:
  # Without managed scaling - added 7:
  regex("^emr-[567]\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$", ...)

  # With managed scaling - added |7\\.[0-9]{1,2}\\.\\d+:
  regex("^emr-(?:5\\.[3-9]\\d+\\.\\d+|6\\.[0-9]{1,2}\\.\\d+|7\\.[0-9]{1,2}\\.\\d+)$", ...)

  ---
  3.3 - Increasing master node disk size

  Problem: /mnt on master node was 96% full (57GB/59GB).

  New variable added to variables.tf:
  variable "master_ebs_volume_size" {
    type    = number
    default = 64  # default 64GB - does not affect other clusters
  }

  ebs_config added to main.tf:
  master_instance_fleet {
    instance_type_configs {
      instance_type = var.master_instance_type
      ebs_config {
        size = var.master_ebs_volume_size
        type = "gp2"
      }
    }
  }

  Set in cluster file:
  master_ebs_volume_size = 128  # 128GB for Hive clusters (heavy log volume)

  ---
  3.4 - Log rotation

  Problem: /mnt/var/log/ was filling up the disk with no retention policy for old logs.

  New bootstrap script modules/emr_new/scripts/configure-logrotate.sh:

  # Block 1: /var/log/ - system logs
  # Uses postrotate + SIGHUP because rsyslog responds to the signal
  /etc/logrotate.d/syslog:
    /var/log/cron, maillog, messages, secure, spooler
    weekly / rotate 3 / maxage 21 / compress / delaycompress

  # Block 2: /mnt/var/log/ - system logs
  # Uses copytruncate because rsyslog also writes here
  /etc/logrotate.d/emr-mnt-logs (part 1):
    /mnt/var/log/cron, maillog, messages, secure, spooler, yum.log, dmesg, grubby
    weekly / rotate 3 / maxage 21 / copytruncate

  # Block 3: /mnt/var/log/ - Java application logs
  # Uses copytruncate because Java keeps file handles open (cannot detect a rename)
  /etc/logrotate.d/emr-mnt-logs (part 2):
    hadoop*, hive*, spark, yarn, hdfs, httpfs, kms, mapreduce,
    hue, livy, mariadb, nginx, oozie, tomcat, zookeeper
    weekly / rotate 3 / maxage 21 / copytruncate / missingok

  Added to main.tf:
  resource "aws_s3_object" "configure-logrotate" {
    bucket = data.aws_s3_bucket.emr.id
    key    = "bootstrapscripts/${var.cluster_name}/configure-logrotate.sh"
    source = "${path.module}/scripts/configure-logrotate.sh"
  }

  bootstrap_action {
    path = "s3://${data.aws_s3_bucket.emr.id}/${aws_s3_object.configure-logrotate.id}"
    name = "configure-logrotate.sh"
  }

  ---
  3.5 - Configuration templates directory

  Old location: etl/common/ (old S3 backend workspace)
  New location: staging/us-east-1/common/ (TFE workspace)

  Files:
  emr-7.12.0-hive-lift_configuration.tpl
  emr-7.12.0-hive-incr-reach_configuration.tpl
  emr-7.12.0-spark-configuration.tpl
  emr-7.12.0-etl-configuration.tpl
  emr-7.12.0-audience-service-etl-configuration.tpl
  emr-7.12.0-rf-dashboard-etl-configuration.tpl
  cluster_configuration.tpl

  ---
  3.6 - Configuration fixes across all templates

  a) HDFS NameNode - /mnt/namenode/current/ filling up

  Added to all templates:
  {
    "Classification": "hdfs-site",
    "Properties": {
      "dfs.namenode.checkpoint.period": "3600",
      "dfs.namenode.checkpoint.txns": "1000000",
      "dfs.namenode.num.checkpoints.retained": "3",
      "dfs.namenode.num.extra.edits.retained": "100000"
    }
  }
  num.extra.edits.retained reduced from default 1,000,000 → 100,000 — the root cause of disk fill-up

  b) Hive warehouse pointing to HDFS

  Added to all templates containing hive-site:
  "hive.metastore.warehouse.dir": "hdfs:///user/hive/warehouse"
  Without this, Hive tables were being written to the master node's local disk instead of HDFS

  c) Spark History cleaner - unified retention

  "spark.history.fs.cleaner.enabled": "true",
  "spark.history.fs.cleaner.maxAge": "7d",    // was 1d in spark template - too aggressive
  "spark.history.fs.cleaner.interval": "1d"

  d) Deprecated Spark API (EMR 7.x / Spark 3.x)

  // Old (removed):
  "spark.blacklist.decommissioning.timeout": "600"

  // New:
  "spark.excludeOnFailure.decommissioning.timeout": "600"

  e) Missing S3 parameter

  Added to files that were missing it:
  "fs.s3.aimd.maxAttempts": "1000"
```

issues.summary: AI skill for provisioning AWS S3 buckets
issues.description:
Terraform module to create AWS S3 bucket — specific rules for AWS S3 bucket.

### Acceptance Criteria:

* IaC engineering standards skill triggers on code reviews, new file creation, and refactoring tasks
* Follows AWS resource naming conventions (references aws-naming skill)
* Follows rules and adopts options defined in organizational module (adgear/terraform-aws-s3-bucket)
* Skill is tested (code generation + resource provisioning on AWS)
